flashcards.front: What is the primary advantage of Liang-Barsky algorithm over Cohen-Sutherland?
flashcards.back: Liang-Barsky is computationally more efficient as it uses parametric equations and processes edges sequentially, requiring fewer endpoint comparisons and bitwise operations.

flashcards.front: What is the year the Liang-Barsky algorithm was developed?
flashcards.back: 1984, developed by You-Dong Liang and Brian Barsky.

flashcards.front: What is the parametric form of a line segment used in Liang-Barsky algorithm?
flashcards.back: x = x1 + u(x2-x1) and y = y1 + u(y2-y1), where u is a parameter in the range [0,1].

flashcards.front: What do the parameters p and q represent in Liang-Barsky algorithm?
flashcards.back: p = (dx, -dx, dy, -dy) represents the direction coefficients; q = (x1-xmin, xmax-x1, y1-ymin, ymax-y1) represents the edge distances.

flashcards.front: What are the four clipping edges in order for Liang-Barsky algorithm?
flashcards.back: Left edge (x=xmin), Right edge (x=xmax), Bottom edge (y=ymin), Top edge (y=ymax).

flashcards.front: When is a line completely outside the clipping window?
flashcards.back: When p=0 and q<0 for any edge (parallel to and outside the clipping edge).

flashcards.front: When is a line completely inside the clipping window?
flashcards.back: When p=0 for all four edges (line is parallel to all clipping edges).

flashcards.front: What values are updated for the entering parameter u1 and exiting parameter u2?
flashcards.back: For p<0, update u1 = max(u1, q/p); For p>0, update u2 = min(u2, q/p).

flashcards.front: What is the condition for a valid clipped line?
flashcards.back: The line is clipped if u1 ≤ u2 after processing all four edges.

flashcards.front: How many comparisons are typically needed in Liang-Barsky vs Cohen-Sutherland?
flashcards.back: Liang-Barsky requires only about 10 comparisons per line, while Cohen-Sutherland may require up to 15-20 endpoint comparisons.

flashcards.front: What type of clipping window does Liang-Barsky algorithm support?
flashcards.back: Liang-Barsky specifically works with axis-aligned rectangular clipping windows.

flashcards.front: What happens to the endpoints after clipping in Liang-Barsky?
flashcards.back: New endpoints are computed as: x = x1 + u(x2-x1) and y = y1 + u(y2-y1) for both u1 and u2 values.

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of Liang-Barsky algorithm?
flashcards.back: O(1) - constant time complexity as it processes exactly 4 edges regardless of line position.

flashcards.front: Why is Liang-Barsky considered a parametric line clipping algorithm?
flashcards.back: It uses parametric equations where the line is expressed as a function of parameter u, and clipping is performed by computing parameter values at intersection points.
